target.name: Clippership
target.founder: Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, Kai Matsuka

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Clippership is a Seed-stage maritime technology company that has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation universe consists of five private peers in the ocean and maritime sector—Standard Subsea, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, Albacore, and Odin Dynamics—with no directly comparable public companies available for benchmarking.

The analysis applies a stage-progression methodology, treating the $5M in total capital raised as a proxy for current implied valuation. Using a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement in this sector, this method yields an implied valuation of $12M. This single triangulation approach generates a range anchored at $12M median, with a low bound of $11M and high bound of $14M, reflecting typical variance around the stage-progression anchor.

The primary constraint in this analysis is methodological: with only one applicable valuation technique and no public comparables, the range rests substantially on the validity of the 2.5x multiplier assumption for maritime technology specifically. Validation would require either additional methodology (comparable transaction multiples or discounted cash flow inputs) or greater transparency into the private peer set's recent financing rounds and valuations. The range of $11M–$14M should be treated as indicative pending deeper market context on Seed-stage maritime venture performance.
